---
# title: "Interactive Visual Analytics for Local Decarbonisation Planning"
# subtitle: "Empowering Policy-Aligned Scenario Exploration"
# author:  "Dany Laksono, Radu Jianu, & Aidan Slingsby"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
   #  logo: "../cgvc2025/assets/citystgeorgelogo.png"
    css: styles.css
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
    footer: "[danylaksono.github.io/presentations/cgvc2025/](danylaksono.github.io/presentations/cgvc2025/)"
    slide-number: c/t
    transition: slide
   #  title-slide-attributes:
   #    data-background-image: ../cgvc2025/assets/citystgeorgelogo.png
   #    # data-background-size: 15%
   #    data-background-position: "98% 5%"
    center: true
    background-transition: fade
    incremental: true
   #  width: 1280
   #  height: 720
---
<!-- 
<h1> Interactive Visual Analytics<br>for Local Decarbonisation Planning </h1>

<h2> Empowering Policy‑Aligned Scenario Exploration </h2>

<hr>

<h3> D. Laksono, R. Jianu, &amp; A. Slingsby </h3>
<h4> City, University of London </h4>

<br>

<h3> Live Demo: <a href="https://decarb-vis.netlify.app">decarb-vis.netlify.app</a> &nbsp;&nbsp; Source: <a href="https://github.com/danylaksono/decarbonisation-glyphmap-planner">GitHub</a></h3> -->
<!-- 
![](../cgvc2025/assets/citystgeorgelogo.png){.absolute top=290 left=1000 width="200"}
![](../cgvc2025/assets/giCentrelogo.webp){.absolute top=425 left=1000 width="200"}
![](../cgvc2025/assets/ai_logo.png){.absolute top=500 left=1010 height="65"} -->


```{=html}
  <div style="min-height:100%; display:flex; justify-content:center; align-items:center; position: relative;">
    <!-- <div class="logo-group-top-right">
      <img src="../cgvc2025/assets/citystgeorgelogo.png" alt="City St George's Logo">
      <img src="../cgvc2025/assets/giCentrelogo.webp" alt="giCentre Logo">
      <img src="../cgvc2025/assets/ai_logo.png" alt="AI Logo">
    </div> -->
    <div style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
      <h1>Interactive Visual Analytics for Local Decarbonisation Planning</h1>
      <p style="font-size: 1.2em;">Empowering Policy-Aligned Scenario Exploration</p>
      <hr>
      <p><strong>Dany Laksono, Radu Jianu & Aidan Slingsby</strong></p>
      <p><small>City St. George's, University of London</small></p>
      </div>
   </div>
```

---


::: {.center}
With the 2050 deadline looming, the UK’s pursuit of its [net zero target]{.highlight-gold} is a contentious journey 
marked by legal and technical challenges, **the delegation of complex decarbonisation decisions to frontline local authorities**, and ongoing debate over the pace and feasibility of the transition.
:::

---

## The Challenge: 

Developing effective and equitable decarbonisation plans is a complex balancing act for UK local authorities.

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### They must balance:

- Technical factors (Carbon reduction)
- Economic factors (Costs, budgets)
- Social factors (Fuel poverty, equity)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### ...exacerbated by:

- Conflicting stakeholder priorities.
- Massive, multivariate datasets.
- No single 'optimal' solution.
:::
::::

<div style="text-align: right;">
  <span class="fragment">This is a <span class="highlight-gold">wicked problem</span> in decision making.</span>
</div>

---

## The Gap in Existing Tools

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Traditional GIS

![](https://placehold.co/400x300/E8F5E9/4CAF50?text=Stacked+Map+Layers)

<figcaption>The 'map layer metaphor' increases cognitive load by forcing users to mentally integrate fragmented information.</figcaption>
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
### 'Black Box' Optimisation

![](https://placehold.co/400x300/F3E5F5/9C27B0?text=Black+Box+Model)

<figcaption>Monolithic optimisation models hide the trade-offs inherent in the planning process.</figcaption>
:::
::::

::: {.notes}
Traditional GIS forces users to flip between layers; black-box optimisation hides trade-offs and the "why" behind results.
:::

---

## Why Simulation, Not Just Optimisation?

For a 'wicked problem', the goal is not a single optimal answer, but to explore a range of plausible and defensible futures.

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### Optimisation

- Asks: "What is the best solution?"
- Finds a single, mathematically optimal result.
- Treats qualitative goals as rigid constraints.
- Hides inherent trade-offs inside the model.
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### Simulation (Our Approach)

- Asks: "If we do X, what happens?"
- Enables multiple what-if scenarios.
- Augments human judgment for complex goals.
- Makes trade-offs explicit and visible.
:::
::::

::: {.notes}
We ask a different question: "If we make this decision, what happens?" It augments expert judgment and makes trade-offs explicit.
:::

---

## Our Contribution: A Human-in-the-Loop Paradigm

We built a visual analytics system on three core contributions to enable this exploratory process.

1. Modular, Component-Based Planning  
   <small>Decomposes complex strategy-building into manageable, interpretable parts.</small>
2. Multi-Scale, Glyph-Based Visualisation  
   <small>Represents multivariate and temporal data uniformly across geographic scales.</small>
3. An Integrated, Iterative Workflow  
   <small>Tightly couples data exploration, simulation, and outcome analysis for real-time feedback.</small>

---

## The Decarbonisation Planner

![](https://placehold.co/800x450/E1F5FE/03A9F4?text=Figure+1:+Dashboard)

<figcaption>The main interface integrates intervention authoring, data tables, a scenario timeline, and a glyph-map for outcome analysis.</figcaption>

---

## A Workflow for Policy Exploration

Our system supports an iterative, four-step process (T1-T4):

1. Identify & Characterise Cohorts (T1)  
   <small>Use linked map and table to filter and select groups of buildings.</small>
2. Author Scenarios via Composition (T2)  
   <small>Define and layer modular plan components on the timeline.</small>
3. Compare Spatio-Temporal Outcomes (T3)  
   <small>Instantly simulate impacts and visualise them on the glyph-map.</small>
4. Explore Alternatives through Iteration (T4)  
   <small>Adjust parameters, re-order components, and immediately see the results.</small>

---

## Visualising Complex Trade-offs with Glyphs

![](https://placehold.co/800x400/FFF3E0/FF9800?text=Figure+3:+Glyph+Designs)

<figcaption>Left: radial glyph for multi-attribute potential. Right: mirrored streamgraph showing Carbon Saved (green) vs. Cost (red) over time.</figcaption>

---

## Managing Scale & Analytical Clarity

![](https://placehold.co/800x450/E8EAF6/3F51B5?text=Figure+2:+Multi-scale+View)

<figcaption>Data is represented consistently across scales. Geography can be 'squarified' to give areas equal visual prominence.</figcaption>

---

## Merits & Limitations

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Merits

- Transparent & interpretable
- Reduces cognitive load
- Fosters iteration & dialogue
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Limitations

- Need for formal evaluation
- Model simplification
- Scalability
:::
::::

---

## Conclusion & Future Work

We reframed decarbonisation planning from a rigid, monolithic process into a transparent, flexible, and iterative dialogue.

Future work:

1. Conduct contextual user studies
2. Enhance simulation fidelity
3. Develop side-by-side scenario comparison

---

## Thank You

**D. Laksono, R. Jianu, & A. Slingsby**

**Live Demo:** <https://decarb-vis.netlify.app>

**Source Code:** <https://github.com/danylaksono/decarbonisation-glyphmap-planner>

---

## Anticipated Q&A {visibility="hidden"}

### Q&A: Glyph Design Rationale

**Q:** Why not use simpler charts like bars?  
**A:** We optimise for at-a-glance assessment of temporal trade-offs across space.

1. Direct comparison via mirrored cost vs carbon saved
2. Focus on trajectory and cumulative nature
3. Space efficiency at scale

---

## Q&A: The Use of Cartograms {visibility="hidden"}

**Q:** Does distorting geography risk misleading the user?  
**A:** We intentionally sacrifice geographic fidelity to gain analytical clarity and perceptual fairness.

- Large, sparse areas can dominate visually
- Grid cartogram gives equal visual prominence
- Users can toggle back to standard view for context

---

## Q&A: Role of Multi-Objective Decision Making {visibility="hidden"}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### MOO & Pareto Fronts

- Computes a set of mathematically optimal solutions
- User explores pre-computed results
- Struggles with qualitative objectives (e.g., equity)
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
#### Our System's Approach

- Facilitates human-led exploration of decision space
- User authors scenarios based on objectives
- Augments the decision-maker, does not automate the decision
:::
::::
